summary: The most operationally significant development is the reported signing of a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon, described by Israeli sources as an accord reached tonight, while a Hezbollah official stated authorities cannot enforce it without a U.S.-backed 'civil war.' Concurrently, the IDF reported eliminating seven Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon near Al-Manzalah who transferred weapons into a structure used as a firing and observation position. Separately, Iran fired at least four one-way attack drones at ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with one striking a large, expensive cargo vessel, which Trump called a 'stupid' ceasefire violation while three drones were shot down.
key_points: Israeli and Lebanese officials are reportedly set to sign a framework agreement tonight, with Israeli sources confirming the deal and Lebanon's ambassador calling it a first step toward restoring Lebanese sovereignty (Source: @yediotnews25/131970).

U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced the framework agreement was signed at the U.S. State Department in Washington, stating it marks progress but 'much work' remains (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4399).
Hezbollah MP Fadlallah told Al-Miyaadin that Lebanese authorities cannot enforce the agreement unless a 'civil war' is opened with U.S. support (Source: @N12chat/214779).

IDF Spokesman released video documentation of the Air Force, directed by the Egoz Unit, eliminating seven Hezbollah terrorists who transferred weapons into a building in the Al-Manzalah area serving as a firing and observation post against IDF forces in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24450).

Trump stated Iran violated the ceasefire by launching at least four one-way attack drones at ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with one directly striking the upper deck of a large, very expensive cargo ship, causing damage but the vessel continued on its way (Source: @almogboker78/17594).
Trump said the U.S. shot down three additional drones and called it a 'stupid violation' of their ceasefire agreement, warning 'you will understand' the response soon (Source: @almogboker78/17594).
IDF reported over 10 targeted killings in Gaza this week, 6 strikes in southern Lebanon, and over 100 arrests in the West Bank (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
Lebanese media reported an IDF drone struck targets in the town of Al-Mansouri in southern Lebanon, with reports of one killed and one seriously wounded (Source: @yediotnews25/131954).

sections.executive_summary: The most operationally significant development is the reported signing of a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon, described by Israeli sources as an accord reached tonight, while a Hezbollah official stated authorities cannot enforce it without a U.S.-backed 'civil war.' Concurrently, the IDF reported eliminating seven Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon near Al-Manzalah who transferred weapons into a structure used as a firing and observation position. Separately, Iran fired at least four one-way attack drones at ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with one striking a large, expensive cargo vessel, which Trump called a 'stupid' ceasefire violation while three drones were shot down.

points: Iran launched at least four one-way attack drones at ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with one directly striking the upper deck of a large, very expensive cargo ship, causing damage but the vessel continued on its way (Source: @almogboker78/17594).
Trump stated the U.S. shot down three additional drones and called it a 'stupid violation' of their ceasefire agreement, warning 'you will understand' the response soon (Source: @almogboker78/17594).
Iranian sources reported explosions in the port city of Sirik in Iran near the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @New_security8200/202104).

Senior adviser to the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ali Akbar Velayati, stated the stability of Persian Gulf Arab states is indebted to Iran's century-long management of the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @presstv/195783).

Iranian missile and drone strikes reportedly inflicted extensive damage on the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, compelling Washington to consider relocating parts of its military assets from Bahrain to bases in the Israeli-occupied Palestinian territories (Source: @presstv/195785).

Iran and the U.S. established a communication channel in the Strait of Hormuz to prevent incidents that could escalate military tensions, with Iran agreeing to send an IRGC force to Doha alongside a CENTCOM representative (Source: @VahidOnline/76689).

Pakistan facilitated the return of 22 Iranian tanker crew members after the U.S. unlawfully seized their tanker in international waters, handing them over to Iranian consular officials in Karachi (Source: @presstv/195790).

IDF Spokesman released video documentation of the Air Force, directed by the Egoz Unit, eliminating seven Hezbollah terrorists who transferred weapons into a building in the Al-Manzalah area serving as a firing and observation post against IDF forces in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24450).
Israeli sources confirmed Israel and Lebanon will sign a framework agreement tonight (Source: @yediotnews25/131970).
IDF reported over 10 targeted killings in Gaza this week, 6 strikes in southern Lebanon, and over 100 arrests in the West Bank (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).

On Thursday, a combat officer was moderately wounded and a combat officer plus two additional fighters were lightly wounded in an encounter with a terrorist in southern Lebanon; they were evacuated to hospital and families were updated (Source: @New_security8200/202088).
Lebanese media reported an IDF drone struck targets in the town of Al-Mansouri in southern Lebanon, with reports of one killed and one seriously wounded (Source: @yediotnews25/131954).
A drone reportedly dropped leaflets in the Al-Mansouri area of southern Lebanon (Source: @yediotnews25/131965).

Israeli Science Minister Gila Gamaliel warned that Turkey is a future threat to Israel, stating that after the passing of Iran's system, the Ottoman Empire with its expansionist ambitions will seek to expand its influence in the region (Source: @kianmeli1/88188).
IDF reported over 10 targeted killings in Gaza during the week (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
IDF reported over 100 arrests in the West Bank (Judea and Samaria) during the week (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
Israeli and Lebanese officials are reportedly set to sign a framework agreement tonight, with Israeli sources confirming the deal (Source: @yediotnews25/131970).

U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced the framework agreement was signed at the U.S. State Department in Washington, stating it marks progress but 'much work' remains (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4399).
Hezbollah MP Fadlallah told Al-Miyaadin that Lebanese authorities cannot enforce the agreement unless a 'civil war' is opened with U.S. support (Source: @N12chat/214779).

Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam stated the goal of the framework agreement is Israel's withdrawal from all Lebanese territory, and Lebanon commits to expanding state control through its security forces (Source: @asafroz/50774).

IDF eliminated seven Hezbollah terrorists who transferred weapons into a building in the Al-Manzalah area serving as a firing and observation post against IDF forces in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24450).
Lebanese media reported an IDF drone struck targets in the town of Al-Mansouri in southern Lebanon, with reports of one killed and one seriously wounded (Source: @yediotnews25/131954).
A drone reportedly dropped leaflets in the Al-Mansouri area of southern Lebanon (Source: @yediotnews25/131965).
Reports indicate the Lebanese government headquarters (Saray) in Beirut was besieged following the agreement between the Lebanese regime and the Israeli entity (Source: @naya_foriraq/80013).
Iran launched at least four one-way attack drones at ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with one directly striking a large, very expensive cargo ship (Source: @almogboker78/17594).

The U.S. and GCC held a joint foreign ministers meeting in Manama on Thursday chaired by U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Bahrain's Foreign Minister Dr. Abdul Latif bin Rashid Al-Zayani (Source: @mamlekate/103763).
Marco Rubio stated that for the U.S., what matters is not the regime's rhetoric on television but the passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @mamlekate/103763).

Iranian missile and drone strikes reportedly inflicted extensive damage on the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, compelling Washington to consider relocating parts of its military assets from Bahrain to bases in the Israeli-occupied Palestinian territories (Source: @presstv/195785).
Emergency alerts were sent to mobile phones in the UAE regarding a possible missile threat (Source: @New_security8200/202093).
There was a malfunction in the UAE's emergency alert system, with no anomaly detected (Source: @lelotsenzura/93238).

key_developments: Israeli and Lebanese officials are reportedly set to sign a framework agreement tonight, with Israeli sources confirming the deal and Lebanon's ambassador calling it a first step toward restoring Lebanese sovereignty (Source: @yediotnews25/131970).

IDF Spokesman released video documentation of the Air Force, directed by the Egoz Unit, eliminating seven Hezbollah terrorists who transferred weapons into a building in the Al-Manzalah area serving as a firing and observation post against IDF forces in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24450).

Iran launched at least four one-way attack drones at ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with one directly striking the upper deck of a large, very expensive cargo ship, causing damage but the vessel continued on its way (Source: @almogboker78/17594).
Trump stated the U.S. shot down three additional drones and called it a 'stupid violation' of their ceasefire agreement, warning 'you will understand' the response soon (Source: @almogboker78/17594).

Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam stated the goal of the framework agreement is Israel's withdrawal from all Lebanese territory, and Lebanon commits to expanding state control through its security forces (Source: @asafroz/50774).
Hezbollah MP Fadlallah told Al-Miyaadin that Lebanese authorities cannot enforce the agreement unless a 'civil war' is opened with U.S. support (Source: @N12chat/214779).

sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is characterized by simultaneous escalation and diplomatic activity across multiple fronts. Iran's drone attack on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz represents a direct challenge to the ceasefire and freedom of navigation, with Trump signaling an imminent response. In southern Lebanon, the IDF's targeted elimination of seven Hezbollah militants in Al-Manzalah indicates ongoing high-intensity counterterrorism operations even as a framework agreement is being signed. The Hezbollah MP's warning that the agreement cannot be opened without a U.S.-backed 'civil war' suggests potential internal Lebanese instability. The reported damage to the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain from Iranian missile and drone strikes raises questions about force posture in the Gulf.
sections.regional_response: The U.S. is pursuing a dual-track approach: diplomatic engagement through the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement signed at the State Department and the U.S.-GCC joint foreign ministers meeting in Manama, alongside military responses including shooting down three Iranian drones. Lebanon's Prime Minister framed the agreement as a step toward Israeli withdrawal from all Lebanese territory and expanded state sovereignty. Iran's senior adviser Velayati rejected Western involvement, asserting Iran's role in managing Gulf stability. The UAE experienced emergency alert disruptions, with a malfunction in the emergency alert system reported.
sections.intelligence_notes: The reported siege of the Lebanese government headquarters (Saray) in Beirut following the agreement suggests potential domestic political friction. The IDF's documentation and release of the Al-Manzalah strike indicates a strategic communications effort to justify operations. Iran's establishment of a communication channel in the Strait of Hormuz with IRGC and CENTCOM representatives in Doha represents a de-escalation mechanism even as drone attacks occurred. The reported damage to the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and potential asset relocation to Israeli-occupied Palestinian territories would represent a significant shift in U.S. force posture if confirmed.
